protocolRunner.interactionHint: This protocol pauses for you to open and close the clamp. Watch the device and act when its lights prompt you.
protocolRunner.runProtocolToSeeResults: Run a protocol to see results
protocolRunner.webBluetoothNotSupported: Web Bluetooth is not supported in this browser
protocolRunner.webSerialNotSupported: Web Serial is not supported in this browser
protocolRunner.deviceNoBLE: This device does not support Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE)
protocolRunner.deviceNoSerial: This device does not support serial connections
protocolRunner.connectToDeviceDescription: Connect to a device and test your protocol before saving
protocolRunner.browserNotSupported: Your browser doesn't support Web Bluetooth or Web Serial APIs.
protocolRunner.tryDifferentBrowser: Try using Chrome, Edge, or Opera on desktop.

devices.title: Devices
devices.description: Register hardware devices as AWS IoT Things. Certificates are issued in a later step.
devices.searchPlaceholder: Search by name or serial number

empty.title: No devices yet
empty.description: Register a device to create its record and an AWS IoT Thing. A certificate is issued in a later step to activate it.

zeroResults.title: No devices match your filters
zeroResults.description: Try a different search term or status filter.

dialog.title: Register device
dialog.description: Register a hardware device as an AWS IoT Thing. No certificate is issued at this stage.
dialog.createSuccessDetail: A certificate will be issued to activate it.

remove.title: Delete device
remove.confirm: This permanently deletes {{name}} and its AWS IoT Thing.

dangerZone.title: Danger zone
dangerZone.deleteDescription: Permanently removes the device and its AWS IoT Thing. This cannot be undone.

credentials.pendingDescription: This device has no certificate yet. Issue one to activate it and let it connect to AWS IoT.
credentials.rotatingDescription: A certificate rotation is in progress.
credentials.revokedDescription: The certificate was revoked, so this device can no longer connect. Issue a new certificate to bring it back online.
credentials.revokeConfirm: This revokes the device certificate immediately. The device will no longer be able to connect. You can issue a new certificate later.
credentials.dialogDescription: Download and install the certificate and key files on the device so it can connect to AWS IoT. They can't be retrieved after you close this.
credentials.showOnceWarning: This is the only time you can download these files. Store them somewhere safe before closing.
credentials.keysDescription: The key files are unique to this certificate and can't be downloaded again after you close this.
credentials.rootCaDescription: Download the root CA that matches your endpoint and cipher suite. You can also download these later.
